mushroom.title: Mushroom
mushroom.excerpt: Exploring how AI is reshaping the analysis and search of mushroom data in offline environments.
mushroom.overview: Mushroom is an AI-powered identification application designed to work entirely offline. Built for foragers and naturalists, it leverages on-device machine learning models to identify mushroom species from photos, providing safety information and habitat data without requiring an internet connection.

highlights.title: On-device inference
highlights.description: A quantized local model runs directly on the device, enabling sub-second identification with no network dependency.

highlights.title: Offline-first database
highlights.description: A curated PostgreSQL database of 2,000+ species includes edibility ratings, look-alike warnings, and seasonal availability.

highlights.title: Safety-first UX
highlights.description: Every identification result surfaces a confidence score and a dedicated warning panel for dangerous look-alikes.

featureMedia.caption: Identification screen — confidence score and look-alike warnings displayed after on-device inference.

learnings: Building truly offline-first apps requires rethinking every dependency — from fonts to model weights.
Quantizing models is a careful trade-off: smaller files mean faster load times but lower accuracy on edge cases.
Mycological datasets are messy; curating and normalising species data took as long as writing the inference pipeline.

url-shortener.title: Url Shortener
url-shortener.excerpt: Building a short URL service with a focus on performance, scalability, and security.
url-shortener.overview: A URL shortening app that generates concise, unique short URLs for long web addresses. The app features an intuitive interface for creating and managing short links, along with robust analytics to track usage.

highlights.title: Idempotent redirects
highlights.description: The service generates the same short URL for the same long URL, preventing duplicates and simplifying analytics.

highlights.title: Tracking of clicks and use
highlights.description: Keep track by custom category of the number of clicks, the date of the last click, and the referrer for each short URL.

learnings: Management of URL collisions is critical, I implemented a retry mechanism with exponential backoff to ensure unique short codes.
User input validation is a must to prevent malicious URLs and ensure the integrity of the service.
Implementing rate limiting and abuse detection early on is crucial to protect against spam and DDoS attacks.

smart-watch.title: SmartWatch
smart-watch.excerpt: Building a smart watch app with a focus on reducing time spent on tech watch or daily information consumption.
smart-watch.overview: A smartwatch app designed to distill the day's most important tech news and updates into a quick, glanceable format. The app uses an agentic architecture to pull in data from multiple sources, summarise it with AI, and present it in a way that respects the constraints of a small screen and short attention span.

highlights.title: Reducing tech watch fatigue
highlights.description: Designing a watch app that surfaces only the most critical updates and insights, helping users stay informed without feeling overwhelmed.

highlights.title: Your daily newsletter
highlights.description: Curate a selection of the day's most important updates and insights into a concise email digest.

learnings: Creating a seamless user experience on a small screen requires a deep understanding of user behavior and intent.
Agentic architectures can help manage the complexity of wearable app development, but they introduce new challenges around state management and error handling.
Balancing information density with readability is key to keeping users engaged without overwhelming them.
